subject: Sports
title: Cryotherapy: The Ice Bath Debate
description: A 60-second lesson on Cryotherapy: The Ice Bath Debate.

hook.text: Athletes sit in tubs of ice water after games. Some swear by it. Science says it's complicated.

buildup.text: Cold water immersion constricts blood vessels, reduces inflammation, and numbs pain after intense exercise.

discovery.text: Studies show ice baths reduce muscle soreness. But they may also blunt muscle adaptation and strength gains.

twist.text: Whole-body cryotherapy chambers (minus 110°C for 3 minutes) became trendy—but evidence for superiority is weak.

climax.text: The best approach may be timing: ice baths after competition, but not after training when you want adaptation.

punchline.text: Ice baths reduce soreness but might slow muscle growth. Timing is everything.

quiz.question: What is a potential downside of ice baths after training?
options: They may blunt muscle adaptation and strength gains
